content.text: Cross-tenant management at scale is the operational model for MSPs using Azure Lighthouse to manage large numbers of customer tenants — often 50 to hundreds — from a single managing tenant. The core challenge shifts from 'can we access the resources' (solved by Lighthouse delegation) to 'how do we efficiently operate at volume': running nightly inventory sweeps, applying policy at scale, triaging alerts from many tenants, generating per-customer cost reports, and escalating access for incident response all without directory switching or manual per-customer steps.

attrs.alt: Lighthouse cross-tenant operations loop: inventory, compliance, patching, alert triage, cost reports, remediation

content.text: The operations loop shown above maps to a cadence: nightly inventory + alert triage, weekly compliance reports, monthly patch maintenance windows and cost reports. Each loop iteration is a scripted or pipeline-driven step — not a manual per-customer action. The goal is O(1) engineer effort regardless of customer count.
Nightly Inventory Sweep with Resource Graph

Resource Graph queries run against the MSP tenant and return results from all delegated customer subscriptions. The MSP SPN has Reader on each delegated scope; the query spans all in one call. Results are stored in a centralized reporting storage account for dashboarding.

# Authenticate as MSP automation SPN
az login --service-principal   --username "$MSP_SPN_APP_ID"   --password "$MSP_SPN_SECRET"   --tenant "$MSP_TENANT_ID"

# Get all delegated subscription IDs (dynamically, from managedServices API)
DELEGATED_SUBS=$(az account list   --query "[?tenantId=='$MSP_TENANT_ID' && state=='Enabled'].id" -o tsv)

# Resource Graph: all VMs across all customers (single query)
az graph query -q "
Resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| extend os = tostring(properties.storageProfile.imageReference.offer)
| extend powerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, os, powerState, location
| order by subscriptionId, name
" --subscriptions $DELEGATED_SUBS -o json > /reports/vm_inventory_$(date +%Y%m%d).json

# Resource Graph: unmanaged disks (cost savings opportunity)
az graph query -q "
Resources
| where type == 'microsoft.compute/disks'
| where isnull(properties.managedBy)
| extend sizeGB = toint(properties.diskSizeGB)
| extend sku = tostring(sku.name)
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, sizeGB, sku
" --subscriptions $DELEGATED_SUBS -o json > /reports/unmanaged_disks_$(date +%Y%m%d).json

# Resource Graph: public IPs with no associated resource
az graph query -q "
Resources
| where type == 'microsoft.network/publicipaddresses'
| where isnull(properties.ipConfiguration)
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location
" --subscriptions $DELEGATED_SUBS -o json > /reports/unused_pips_$(date +%Y%m%d).json

# Upload reports to MSP centralized storage
az storage blob upload-batch   --destination "nightly-reports"   --source "/reports/"   --pattern "*.json"   --account-name $MSP_STORAGE_ACCOUNT

Every week the MSP pipeline generates a per-customer compliance summary: number of non-compliant resources, which policies are failing, and which resource groups have the most violations. The report is stored as JSON and emailed to the customer account team.

REPORT_WEEK=$(date +%Y-W%V)

for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  CUSTOMER_NAME=$(az account show --subscription "$SUB" --query "name" -o tsv)
  echo "Generating compliance report for: $CUSTOMER_NAME ($SUB)"

  # Count non-compliant resources per policy
  az policy state list     --subscription "$SUB"     --filter "complianceState eq 'NonCompliant'"     --select "resourceId,policyDefinitionName,resourceGroup"     --query "[].{resource:resourceId, policy:policyDefinitionName, rg:resourceGroup}"     -o json > "/reports/${SUB}_compliance_${REPORT_WEEK}.json"

  # Count by policy
  NON_COMPLIANT=$(az policy state list --subscription "$SUB"     --filter "complianceState eq 'NonCompliant'"     --query "length(@)" -o tsv)

  # Count by severity (critical policies)
  CRITICAL=$(az policy state list --subscription "$SUB"     --filter "complianceState eq 'NonCompliant' and policySetDefinitionCategory eq 'Security'"     --query "length(@)" -o tsv)

  echo "{"subscription":"$SUB","customer":"$CUSTOMER_NAME","week":"$REPORT_WEEK","nonCompliant":$NON_COMPLIANT,"securityNonCompliant":$CRITICAL}"     >> /reports/weekly_summary_${REPORT_WEEK}.jsonl

  # Trigger immediate remediation for auto-remediable policies
  az policy remediation create     --name "weekly-remediation-$REPORT_WEEK"     --policy-assignment "deploy-ama-all-machines"     --resource-group "$(az group list --subscription "$SUB" --query "[0].name" -o tsv)"     --subscription "$SUB"     --no-wait 2>/dev/null || true

done

# Send summary report via Logic App webhook (email to customer teams)
curl -s -X POST "$LOGIC_APP_WEBHOOK_URL"   -H "Content-Type: application/json"   --data-binary @/reports/weekly_summary_${REPORT_WEEK}.jsonl

MSP Network Operations Centers (NOCs) use Azure Monitor Workbooks configured in the managing tenant to surface alerts from all delegated customer subscriptions. Workbooks in the managing tenant can reference Log Analytics workspaces and alert resources across customer subscriptions using the MSP's Lighthouse-delegated Monitoring Reader role.

# Create a cross-customer alert summary workbook (ARM resource in MSP tenant)
# The workbook queries alerts from multiple customer subscriptions

WORKBOOK_BODY='{
  "kind": "shared",
  "properties": {
    "displayName": "Cross-Customer Alert Triage",
    "category": "workbook",
    "serializedData": "{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"AlertsManagementResources | where type == 'microsoft.alertsmanagement/alerts' | where properties.essentials.severity in ('Sev0','Sev1','Sev2') | where properties.essentials.alertState == 'New' | project subscriptionId, name, properties.essentials.alertRule, properties.essentials.severity, properties.essentials.targetResource | order by properties_essentials_severity asc","crossComponentResources":["'$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_A'","'$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_B'","'$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_C'"],"queryType":1,"resourceType":"microsoft.resourcegraph/resources"}}]}"
  }
}'

az rest --method PUT   --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$MSP_SUB_ID/resourceGroups/msp-monitoring-rg/providers/Microsoft.Insights/workbooks/$WORKBOOK_GUID?api-version=2022-04-01"   --body "$WORKBOOK_BODY"

# KQL: cross-tenant alert summary for NOC dashboard (run in MSP portal)
# AlertsManagementResources
# | where type == "microsoft.alertsmanagement/alerts"
# | where properties.essentials.severity in ("Sev0","Sev1")
# | where properties.essentials.alertState == "New"
# | extend customer = tostring(split(subscriptionId, "-")[0])  // map to customer name
# | extend rule = tostring(properties.essentials.alertRule)
# | extend resource = tostring(properties.essentials.targetResource)
# | project subscriptionId, customer, rule, resource, properties.essentials.severity
# | order by properties_essentials_severity asc

# KQL: identify customers with most open Sev1+ alerts (for triage priority)
# AlertsManagementResources
# | where type == "microsoft.alertsmanagement/alerts"
# | where properties.essentials.severity in ("Sev0","Sev1")
# | where properties.essentials.alertState == "New"
# | summarize alertCount=count() by subscriptionId
# | order by alertCount desc
Monthly Patch Maintenance Across All Customers

Azure Update Manager maintenance configurations apply patch schedules to entire fleets. An MSP creates one maintenance configuration per customer (or per OS type per customer) and assigns all eligible Arc machines and Azure VMs to it. The maintenance window runs at a customer-agreed time; the MSP monitors patch completion from the managing tenant.

# Create per-customer maintenance configurations (run once per customer)
for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  CUSTOMER_NAME=$(az account show --subscription "$SUB" --query "name" -o tsv | tr ' ' '-' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')

  az maintenance configuration create     --resource-group "msp-operations-rg"     --subscription "$SUB"     --name "${CUSTOMER_NAME}-monthly-patch"     --maintenance-scope InGuestPatch     --location eastus     --start-date-time "2024-04-02 02:00"     --duration "04:00"     --recur-every "Month Second Tuesday"     --time-zone "UTC"     --reboot-setting IfRequired

done

# Assign VMs to maintenance configurations (bulk assignment per customer sub)
for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  CUSTOMER_NAME=$(az account show --subscription "$SUB" --query "name" -o tsv | tr ' ' '-' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
  MC_ID=$(az maintenance configuration show     --subscription "$SUB" -g msp-operations-rg     -n "${CUSTOMER_NAME}-monthly-patch" --query id -o tsv)

  # Assign all Azure VMs in this sub
  az vm list --subscription "$SUB" --query "[].{id:id}" -o tsv |   while read VM_ID; do
    VM_NAME=$(basename "$VM_ID")
    VM_RG=$(az resource show --ids "$VM_ID" --query resourceGroup -o tsv)
    az maintenance assignment create       --resource-group "$VM_RG"       --subscription "$SUB"       --location eastus       --name "${VM_NAME}-mc"       --maintenance-configuration-id "$MC_ID"       --resource-id "$VM_ID"       --no-wait 2>/dev/null || true
  done
done

# After patch window: check patch completion per customer
for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  echo "=== Patch results: $SUB ==="
  az vm list --subscription "$SUB"     --query "[].name" -o tsv | head -5 | while read VM; do
    RG=$(az vm show --subscription "$SUB" -n "$VM" --query resourceGroup -o tsv)
    az vm get-instance-view --subscription "$SUB" -g "$RG" -n "$VM"       --query "instanceView.patchStatus.lastPatchInstallationSummary.{result:status,installed:installedPatchCount,failed:failedPatchCount}"       -o json 2>/dev/null
  done
done
MSP SLA Reporting: Health and Uptime Metrics

MSPs often commit to SLAs in managed service contracts. Azure Monitor can generate availability metrics for customer workloads from the managing tenant. SLA reports are built from Monitor availability tests, Resource Graph health checks, and Log Analytics heartbeat queries.
Resource Graph query times out on 200+ customer subs
Single query spanning too many subscriptions
Batch into groups of 50 subs; paginate with --skip-token
Policy remediation tasks queue up across many customers
ARM throttling on concurrent remediation creates
Stagger remediation task creation; retry with exponential backoff
Alert noise from many tenants overwhelms NOC
No alert deduplication or severity filtering
Create action group rules to suppress P3/P4; funnel P1/P2 only to PagerDuty
Cost Management exports take too long for 100 subs
Parallelize with az --no-wait; collect results asynchronously
SPN token expires during long-running script
Re-authenticate via az account get-access-token --renew before each sub iteration
New customer subscription not picked up in automation
Use az account list filtered by Lighthouse delegation instead of a static list
Incident Response with JIT PIM Across Customer Tenants

When an incident requires elevated access in a customer tenant, the MSP engineer activates their eligible authorization (PIM) for that customer's scope. The activation window is time-bounded and logged. For multi-customer incidents (e.g., a vulnerability affecting all customers), the engineer activates PIM for each affected customer scope sequentially.

# MSP engineer: find eligible assignments across all delegated customer subs
az extension add --name pim

# List all eligible role assignments for the current engineer
# (eligible = inactive JIT roles that can be activated)
az pim eligible-assignment list   --assignee "$(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv)"   --include-groups true   --query "[].{scope:properties.scope, role:properties.expandedProperties.roleDefinition.displayName,                expiry:properties.scheduleInfo.expiration.endDateTime}" -o table

# Activate Contributor role for customer A (4-hour window, with justification)
ELIGIBLE_ASSIGNMENT_ID="<ID from list above for customer-A sub>"
az pim assignment activate   --id "$ELIGIBLE_ASSIGNMENT_ID"   --justification "INC-20240315: P1 disk corruption on customer-a-vm-prod-01"   --duration PT4H

# Confirm activation
az pim assignment list   --assignee "$(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv)"   --query "[?properties.status=='Active'].{role:properties.expandedProperties.roleDefinition.displayName, scope:properties.scope}"

# Respond to incident: restart affected VM in customer A
az vm restart   --subscription "$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_A"   --resource-group "prod-rg"   --name "customer-a-vm-prod-01"

# Deactivate elevation early when incident is resolved (optional — expires automatically)
az pim assignment deactivate   --id "$ELIGIBLE_ASSIGNMENT_ID"

# Post-incident: verify all MSP actions in customer A Activity Log
az monitor activity-log list   --subscription "$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_A"   --start-time "$(date -u -d '4 hours ago' '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')"   --query "[?caller contains '@contoso-msp.com'].{time:eventTimestamp,op:operationName.localizedValue,resource:resourceId}"   -o table | head -20
Customer Reporting Dashboard with Azure Workbooks

MSPs build customer-facing dashboards using Azure Workbooks in the managing tenant. The workbook is parameterized by customer (subscription ID), pulling data from the customer's Log Analytics workspace and Resource Graph. The MSP shares a read-only link with the customer using a Workbook shared link — customers see their own data without needing Azure portal access.

Cross-tenant management at scale operationalizes Azure Lighthouse across dozens to hundreds of customer tenants using automation, pipelines, and consolidated views. The MSP operations hub in the managing tenant runs nightly Resource Graph inventory sweeps, weekly compliance reports, monthly patch maintenance windows, and continuous alert triage — all without directory switching. Service principal automation handles the cadenced work; engineers use JIT PIM elevation only for incident response. Workbooks in the managing tenant surface cross-customer NOC dashboards; Cost Management exports feed per-customer billing reports. Key scalability practices: dynamic subscription lists from Lighthouse delegation metadata, batched Resource Graph queries, staggered remediation task creation, and centralized storage for all cross-tenant reports.
